title: T. Rex Bones Reveal Hidden Blood Vessels!
summary: Scientists found blood vessels inside the bones of a T. rex named Scotty. Usually, only hard parts like bones and teeth are found from dinosaurs. But sometimes, soft parts like blood vessels can be preserved. This is very rare and exciting because it tells us more about how dinosaurs lived. Scotty was a huge T. rex that lived 66 million years ago. It had a broken rib that was healing. When bones heal, more blood vessels grow there. Scientists used special X-rays to see inside the bone without breaking it. They saw tiny blood vessels that were turned into minerals. This helps us understand how dinosaurs healed from injuries. The discovery shows that even after millions of years, we can still find traces of dinosaur soft parts.

keywords.explanation: A very large meat-eating dinosaur that lived long ago.
Tubes in the body that carry blood.
The remains of a plant or animal that turned into rock.
Natural substances like rock that replace the original material in fossils.
A bone in the chest area.

What happens to soft parts when a fossil forms?

background_read: Dinosaurs lived millions of years ago. Their bones and teeth sometimes become fossils. Fossils are like rocks that show the shape of the original bone. Scientists study fossils to learn about dinosaurs. Usually, only hard parts become fossils. But sometimes, soft parts like blood vessels can also be preserved. This is very rare. Finding blood vessels in a T. rex bone helps scientists understand how dinosaurs healed and lived.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and a T. rex named Scotty.
WHAT: Scientists found blood vessels in Scotty's bone.
WHERE: The bone is from the Royal Saskatchewan Museum in Canada.
WHY: The blood vessels were preserved because the rib was healing from a break.

perspectives.description: Excited to find soft tissues in dinosaur fossils.
Learn that new technology can reveal hidden details.
